name: The Sierra de Ricote and the Navela — Circular via the Collado de la Madera Trail (PR-MU 47)
name_original: La Sierra de Ricote y la Navela — Circular por el Sendero del Collado de la Madera (PR-MU 47)
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails around Sierra de Ricote traverse a landscape characterized by diverse natural and rural features, including mountains, cliffs, and fertile orchards. The Segura River flows through the valley, contributing to its lushness, contrasting with the surrounding arid mountains. Hikers can experience varied terrains, from pine forests and rosemary bushes to rocky areas and badlands. The chalky mountains provide a distinct backdrop for the trails, which often follow picturesque paths along the riverbanks. Best dog friendly hiking trails around Sierra de Ricote The most popular dog friendly hiking route is View of Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Trail of the Moriscos loop from Ricote, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Azud de Ojós Reservoir and follows historical paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail of the Moriscos – View of Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Ricote, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail provides a shorter option to explore the Moriscos trail and reservoir views. Local hikers also love the View of the Ricote Valley – View of the Sierra de la Pila loop from Ricote, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through the Ricote Valley, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Sierra de Ricote is defined by chalky mountains, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate climbs. The routes in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 240 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra de Ricote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on dog-friendly hikes in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The Sierra de Ricote offers a diverse range of terrains suitable for you and your dog. You'll find paths winding through pine forests, rosemary bushes, and rocky areas, as well as picturesque walks along the Segura River. Some trails also feature unique badlands and chalky mountains, providing varied landscapes for exploration.

Q: Are there any easy dog-friendly circular routes in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular routes perfect for a relaxed walk with your dog. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31412224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Moriscos – View of Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Ricote&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.5 km path with gentle elevation. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ricote Valley – View of the Sierra de la Pila loop from Ricote&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 6.8 km and also considered easy.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking with my dog in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a small oasis with natural pools, or enjoy panoramic views from various viewpoints. The Segura River itself offers lush scenery, contrasting with the more arid surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging dog-friendly hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more challenging adventure with their canine companion, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10324732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Trail of the Moriscos loop from Ricote&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 12 km route involves a greater elevation gain, offering a more strenuous workout while still being dog-friendly.

Q: What do other hikers say about the dog-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Sierra de Ricote, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the routes, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical elements with their dogs.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the dog-friendly trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Ricote is known for its stunning viewpoints. Many trails offer vistas of the orchards, the Segura River, and the surrounding mountains. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de la Pila&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ricote Valley from Collado de la Calera&lt;/a&gt;, providing excellent photo opportunities for you and your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The Sierra de Ricote is enjoyable for hiking year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for you and your dog. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making longer walks more comfortable and allowing you to fully appreciate the lushness of the valley or the vibrant autumn colors.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the dog-friendly routes?
A: The Ricote Valley is steeped in history, with Moorish heritage evident in its ancient irrigation systems and Roman sites, particularly around Fuente Caputa. While specific routes might not always pass directly through these, the general area offers a blend of outdoor activity with cultural exploration, often near historic towns.

Q: Are there any specific rules or considerations for dogs on the trails?
A: While the trails are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Always carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, and be sure to clean up after your pet to maintain the natural beauty of the area for everyone.

Q: Can I find trails that offer a mix of forest and river views for my dog?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Sierra de Ricote combine the tranquility of pine forests with the refreshing presence of the Segura River. These routes provide varied scenery and opportunities for your dog to enjoy different smells and textures, from shaded woodland paths to riverside strolls.
tips: Nice trail through the Sierra de Ricote and La Navela. By sectors it can be somewhat technical but it can be avoided without problems, you can even take the variants marked as PR-MU-47.1 or Senda Bermeja. Remember to wear suitable footwear and plenty of water, it is an uninhabited area and there will be no fountain.
